CBS News Live
                      
        
        
                                                CBS News latest headlines
                                    

                          
        
          
        

        Nov 26, 2014
          
          
                          CBS News Live

2022 primary election results
                    

        
        
          The results are trickling in after five states held primary elections Tuesday. CBS News elections and surveys director Anthony Salvanto joined Vladimir Duthiers and Anne-Marie Green with a look at how things stand in a few of the closely-watched races.
        

        4H ago
          
          
                          05:08

Congress holds first UFO hearing since 1960s
                    

        
        
          Congress held its first hearing on UFOs, or unidentified flying objects, in over 50 years. As lawmakers stressed this as a potential national security issue, officials from the Pentagon testified yesterday that reports of potential unexplained encounters rose from 144 in 2004-2021 to approximately 400 since then. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.
        

        6H ago
          
          
                          02:00

Amber Heard's final testimony day
                    

        
        
          Attorneys for Johnny Depp question Amber Heard's motives and veracity as she takes the stand for the last time in her ex-husband's defamation trial.
        

        6H ago
          
          
                          02:16

Discord posts reveal alleged shooter's plans
                    

        
        
          CBS News has reviewed more than 600 pages of messages the suspected Buffalo gunman is believed to have written on the social media platform Discord. They reveal a trove of racist, antisemitic posts and a detailed attack plan described as "a public service." Jeff Pegues reports.
        

        6H ago
          
          
                          02:39

Takeaways from Tuesday's primaries
                    

        
        
          Trump's high-profile endorsements had mixed results on Tuesday night and Republicans advancing candidates who questioned the eleciton results.
        

        11H ago
Madison Cawthorn, Trump-backed incumbent congressman, loses primary
Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Kentucky, Idaho and Oregon primary results

Watch Live: Testimony continues in Johnny Depp-Amber Heard libel trial
                    

        
        
          Depp's attorney Camille Vasquez finished her cross-examination of Heard on Tuesday afternoon.

          Thomas Lane will receive a three-year sentence, which will be served in a federal institution, according to his attorney.

Russian soldier accused of war crimes pleads guilty to killing unarmed civilian
                    

        
        
          Prosecutors said the 21-year-old Russian soldier was ordered to shoot a 62-year-old civilian riding a bicycle to stop him from sharing their location.
        

        4H ago

Finland says it could fend off Russian aggression with or without NATO
                    

        
        
          A top Finnish diplomat tells CBS News his country would bolster the transatlantic alliance, but even without membership, Finland has its own "credible deterrence."
        

        6H ago
Finland and Sweden officially apply to join NATO

U.S. and world court step up efforts to document war crimes in Ukraine
                    

        
        
          As the ICC sends its "largest ever" team into Ukraine, the Biden administration launches a program to preserve and analyze open-source evidence of atrocities.
        

        5H ago

Russian gymnast gets 1-year ban for wearing pro-war symbol "Z"
                    

        
        
          Ivan Kuliak taped the "Z" symbol his vest for a medal ceremony at a World Cup event in March.
        

        6H ago

        5H ago

          The former athlete is now an author and motivational speaker with a following of more than 50 million.

Person to Person with Norah O'Donnell
                      
        
        
                          Norah O’Donnell Interviews Katy Perry
                    

                          
        
          "Person to Person" with Norah O’Donnell brings us the most intimate type of interview — going beyond the headlines through thoughtful conversation. In this episode, O’Donnell sits down with Katy Perry to talk about her career and residency in Las Vegas. The superstar opens up about motherhood, life before fame, and what’s next for her.
        

        May 3
          
          
                          23:02

Norah O'Donnell interviews "Ted Lasso" cast
                    

        
        
          "Person to Person with Norah O'Donnell" brings viewers the most intimate type of interview, going beyond the headlines through thoughtful conversation. In this episode, O'Donnell sits down with the cast of "Ted Lasso" for often laugh-out-loud-funny interviews. She speaks with Hannah Waddingham and Brett Goldstein about the show's success and what they bring to their characters and attends a soccer match in West London with breakout stars Brendan Hunt, Toheeb Jimoh and Kola Bokinni.
        

        Apr 5
          
          
                          21:30

Norah O'Donnell interviews Robin Arzón
                    

        
        
          "Person to Person" with Norah O'Donnell brings viewers the most intimate type of interview, going beyond the headlines through thoughtful conversation. In this episode, O'Donnell sits down with Peloton's head instructor Robin Arzón to talk about her fitness journey, near-death experience, motherhood and more. Arzón brings to "Person to Person" the inspiration she's known for on the bike and the treadmill.
        

        Feb 22
          
          
                          23:49

Norah O'Donnell interviews Brené Brown
                    

        
        
          "Person to Person" with Norah O'Donnell brings viewers the most intimate type of interview — going beyond the headlines through thoughtful conversation. In this episode, O'Donnell sits down with best-selling author Brené Brown about her new book "Atlas of the Heart," which maps out the language people use to describe emotions. The two discuss vulnerability as a strength and why the ability to identify feelings can give someone power over their emotions.
        

        Jan 25
          
          
                          24:06

Biden visits supermarket shooting memorial
                    

        
        
          President Biden visited a Black community in mourning over a racially motivated supermarket shooting in Buffalo. In remarks, Mr. Biden said the nation must not remain silent in the face of racist conspiracy theories as doing so is "complicity." Jericka Duncan has more.

CBS Reports
                      
        
        
                          Are the Kids All Right?: Free Speech
                    

                          
        
          Is free speech under threat? CBS Reports explores that question with members of Gen Z, many of whom say their right to free expression is eroding.
        

        May 12
          
          
                          24:11

Are the Kids All Right?: The Internet
                    

        
        
          Gen Z is the first generation to go from birth through adolescence completely connected. This episode of the CBS Reports documentary series "Are the Kids All Right?" reveals the toll the online world is taking on kids’ self-esteem and self-control, as well as how some intrepid teens are trying to reshape their generation's relationship with technology.
        

        May 5
          
          
                          23:12

Are the Kids All Right?: Racism
                    

        
        
          A racial reckoning is happening in America, but the voices of kids and teens are often missing from that conversation. In this episode of the CBS Reports documentary series "Are the Kids All Right?" young people from across the country share their perspectives on race and firsthand experiences with racism in the U.S. today.
        

        Apr 28
          
          
                          24:37

Are the Kids All Right?: Chasing Fame
                    

        
        
          "Chasing Fame" is the first episode of a new CBS Reports documentary series that explores the transformational shifts faced by today's youth — in their own words — to answer the question: "Are the Kids All Right?" "Chasing Fame" looks at the toll chasing social media fame can take on teens.
        

        Apr 21

                          Lucius dances out of darkness with new album
                    

                          
        
          Indie pop band Lucius is out with their fourth studio album, Second Nature, after lead singers Holly Laessig and Jess Wolfe took time away from Lucius to work as some of the most in-demand backup vocalists for major acts.
